sharc
Documentation développeur
Scope

This file is for OpenCode agents working in the sharc repository (ShArc, ArcGeoSim, Arcane-based two-phase flow simulations).

Only keep in mind what is non-obvious but enforced by the code/config.

Build & Install

  • Root build system is CMake + ArcGeoSim/ArcaneInfra. Do not add a standalone CMake project or alternative build tool.
  • The top-level CMakeLists.txt assumes the ArcGeoSim framework is present under common/ as a git submodule:
    • If ${ARCGEOSIM_FRAMEWORK_ROOT}/ArcaneInfra/build-system/arcgeosim/LoadArcGeoSim.cmake is missing, CMake fails with a message instructing git submodule update --init.
  • ARCGEOSIM_FRAMEWORK_ROOT controls where ArcGeoSim is loaded from:
    • If unset, it defaults to ${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}/common and uses ${PROJECT_BINARY_DIR}/common for framework binaries.
    • If set (or via ENV{ARCGEOSIM_FRAMEWORK_ROOT}), ShArc treats ArcGeoSim as external and uses ${PROJECT_BINARY_DIR} for framework binaries.
  • Required CMake modules and macros (e.g. createLibrary, createExecutable, generateCMakeLists, ARCANE_ADD_TEST_*) come from ArcaneInfra/build-system/arcgeosim. Do not reimplement them locally.
  • The build enables specific metas:
    • strong_options, alien20, arcane3 via loadMeta(...) + -DUSE_ARCANE_V3. Respect these options when adding new packages/tests.
  • Installation layout is controlled by GNUInstallDirs with CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR forced to lib. If you add new install rules, follow this convention.

Typical build commands (verified from README)

  • Standalone ShArc (Ubuntu 24.04 example, assuming Arcane framework already installed into $INSTALL_DIR):
    • Configure:
      • cmake -S $PWD/sharc -B $PWD/build-sharc-ubuntu2404 \ -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \ -DDOTNET_BUILD_TYPE=Release \ -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=ON \ -DARCANEFRAMEWORK_ROOT=${INSTALL_DIR} \ -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=mpicc -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=mpic++ ...
    • Build:
      • cmake --build $PWD/build-sharc-ubuntu2404

Do not change these flows lightly; they are part of the documented installation guide.

Project Structure & Entry Points

  • Root CMakeLists.txt orchestrates components:
    • Loads ArcGeoSim via include(LoadArcGeoSim) and optional loadPackage(NAME ArcGeoSim).
    • Adds subdirectories in this order: src/CAWF, src (ShArc library), src/Main (ShArc.exe), test.
  • Main executable:
    • src/Main/CMakeLists.txt creates ShArc.exe with main.cc and links:
      • ShArc (main library)
      • ArcGeoSim components (either ArcGeoSim::arcgeosim or the per-component libraries)
      • Optional solver/infra targets (alien, intel).
  • Core library:
    • src/CMakeLists.txt defines library ShArc and calls generateCMakeLists(ShArc) and generateGumpModel(TwoPhaseFlowSimulation/DataModel/ArcRes.gump).
    • It links arcres, ArcGeoSim mesh/physics/tests (for Windows), and CAWF.
    • Any new ShArc code should be wired through these ArcGeoSim helpers instead of raw CMake targets.
  • ArcGeoSim sub-framework lives under common/ArcGeoSim and is built when ArcGeoSim_FOUND is false. Do not move this directory; the build scripts assume its path.

Tests: Structure & Invocation

  • All tests are registered via Arcane macros in test/CMakeLists.txt.
  • Macros in use:
    • ARCANE_ADD_TEST_SEQUENTIAL(name target path arcfile)
    • ARCANE_ADD_TEST_PARALLEL(name target path arcfile np)
    • ARCANE_ADD_TEST_SEQUENTIAL_LONGTIME(name target path arcfile timeout)
  • ShArc test suite (as of current CMakeLists.txt):
    • Two-phase flow tests under test/TwoPhaseFlowSimulation:
      • Sequential and parallel variants, including long-running SPE10 cases.
    • ExaDiBench tests under test/ExaDiBench (bench-cube3d.arc, with sequential and parallel variants).
  • Longtime tests (*_SEQUENTIAL_LONGTIME) use a timeout parameter (e.g. 600). Expect these to be slow; avoid enabling them in tight feedback loops unless explicitly needed.
  • MPI/parallel tests rely on the MPI configuration used at CMake configure time (mpicc, mpic++ in the README example). If you change compilers, ensure MPI settings stay coherent.
  • Once CMake is configured, test execution is standard CTest:
    • From the build tree: ctest or ctest -R <TestName>.
  • For installed setups using common/ArcaneInfra/bin/install_files, the recommended flow from README.txt is:
    • Configure: ../bin/cmake ..
    • Run tests: ../bin/ctest
    • This expects a data subdirectory containing the test input tree.

High-Cost / Special Tests

  • test/LargeScaleTwoPhaseFlowSimulation/README.md documents a very large 3D case (~41.9e6 cells, up to ~713e6 cells):
    • Uses .arc files with __NPX__x__NPY__x__NPZ__-__NP__p patterns and a helper genPNXY.py to generate concrete .arc files.
    • These are benchmark-scale tests; do not run them by default in CI or quick verification.
    • If you need to run them, generate the .arc file via genPNXY.py as documented there.

ArcGeoSim / Arcane-Specific Quirks

  • Many CMake commands are domain-specific (e.g. generateGumpModel, loadMeta, logStatus, commit, createLibrary, createExecutable). They come from ArcGeoSim/ArcaneInfra and assume certain conventions:
    • New libraries/executables should use these macros rather than raw add_library/add_executable, otherwise you risk breaking framework integration and install/export behavior.
  • Environment variables and options:
    • Prerequisite docs reference ARCANEFRAMEWORK_ROOT, ARCGEOSIM_FRAMEWORK_ROOT, and various *_DIR CMake package locations. Changing these requires understanding Arcane’s packaging; avoid casual edits.
  • Some tools (build-system C# helpers, ApplicationLauncher, ArcaneInfra.Tools) are .NET/Mono-based and are treated as prebuilt binaries:
    • Do not delete or rename these binaries; they are consumed by the build system.
    • If you need to rebuild them, follow common/ArcaneInfra/build-system/csharp/README.md and ApplicationLauncher/README.

Submodules & External Dependencies

  • The root CMake explicitly checks for ArcaneInfra/build-system/arcgeosim/LoadArcGeoSim.cmake and instructs to run:
    • git submodule update --init
    • Agents should not bypass this by copying files; fix the submodule state instead.
  • The top-level README’s installation section is the authoritative source for system-level dependencies (Boost, HDF5, MPI, Trilinos, PETSc, Hypre, dotnet, etc.). When adding/adjusting dependencies, cross-check with that file rather than inventing new ones.

When Modifying Tests or Adding New Ones

  • Use existing Arcane macros (ARCANE_ADD_TEST_*) and mirror the patterns in test/CMakeLists.txt.
  • Keep test paths and .arc filenames consistent with existing cases; they often encode solver configuration and grid size.
  • For parallel tests, pass an explicit process count (last argument of ARCANE_ADD_TEST_PARALLEL). Do not rely on defaults.

Other Notes for Agents

  • This repo is tightly coupled to the Arcane/ArcGeoSim ecosystem. Before refactoring build logic or core types, search in common/ArcGeoSim and ArcaneInfra to understand how they are expected to behave.
  • Prefer reading and following the Arcane/ArcGeoSim CMake helpers over adding custom CMake functions in this repo.

OpenCode Configuration

  • As of now there is no repo-local OpenCode config (opencode.json or .opencode/).
  • If such config is added later, agents should:
    • Read opencode.json and files under .opencode/ first, then this AGENTS.md.
    • Treat those files as the primary source of per-repo instructions and workflows.
  • Generated by 1.9.1
